scenario_results.reason: Complete franchise training evidence was treated as in-scope and translated into an enablement readiness summary and controlled go-live handoff.
Missing sandbox access, assessment, MFA, and field-trainer records produced a blocked status with explicit remediation requirements.
Go-live readiness was refused when training evidence was unavailable and blocker evidence was documented rather than inferred.
Training readiness was separated from steady-state coaching and routed with a boundary-safe handoff.
Premature production admin access was detected as a permission mismatch and escalated to identity administration.
Generic onboarding was rejected as insufficient because franchise-specific Item 11, operations-manual, brand-standard, and cohort records were missing.
The lane refused to flatten franchise training into generic orientation and preserved industry-specific records and controls.
Legal and training-waiver authority was refused and routed to franchise legal or registration compliance.
Missing tenant facts and systems-of-record context triggered a low-confidence hold with explicit required facts.
Conflicting FDD and tenant shortcut policies were surfaced as an escalation instead of being synthesized into a confident answer.
Tenant-specific controls tightened the workflow without displacing the public FTC, NASAA, IFA, IACET, and NIST baseline.
Franchise-specific terminology and artifacts were handled directly, preventing generic training administration behavior.

notes: Scenario suite covers the queue-level must-pass families for setup blockers, unsupported go-live requests, and ambiguous ownership between onboarding and steady-state support.
All required shared, onboarding-phase, and cross-industry abilities are mapped to passing scenarios with complete coverage.
